subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Liquification of liquation: In this method a low melting metal like tin can be made to flow on a sloping surface. In this way it is separated from higher melting impurities.

This process is used for the purification of the metal in which melting point of the metal to be purified should be lower than that of each of the impurities associated with the metal. This process is used for the purification of Sn and Zn, and for removing Pb from Zn-Ag alloy.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
According to the question, In the first step of the reaction, when cyclo-pentyl-chloride reacts with magnesium in the presence of dry ether than cyclo-pentyl-magnesium-chloride (grignard reagent) is formed. 

In the second step of the reaction, when grignard reagent is react with a compound, which has active hydrogen than grignard reagent abstract hydrogen from active hydrogen compound and converted in to alkane. 

So cyclo-pentane is formed as the product(second step of the reaction) of this reaction.

Therefore, Equation of plane when D.R of parallel plane is given is,x-12=y+23=z-3-6=λ

⇒x=2λ+1 , y=3λ-2  , z=-6λ+3

since, x-y+z=5

⇒2λ+1-3λ+2-6λ+3=5

⇒λ=17

⇒x=97 , y=-117 , z=157

Distance between points 1,-2,3&97,-117,157=272+372+-672=1 unit

solution: The relation between the energy of photons and the intensity of photons is, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>h</mi><mi>v</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>I</mi><mi>A</mi></mrow><mi>n</mi></mfrac></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> is the number of photons, if we increase the number of photons then frequency does not change it means total energy and kinetic energy of photon remains unchanged but due to to this property the intensity of light is increased.
